author: steve

patch.value: GitHub issue: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-changelog/issues/6\n\nFix: added compareVersionHeadings/compareVersionStrings to groupSectionsByMetadata in src/generator.ts.
Changelog sections out of version order when items have different updated_at times
When using --group-by release, sections were ordered by when the first item was last updated, not by release version. Fixed by sorting sections by version heading descending after grouping.
Changelog sections appear in descending version order regardless of item update timestamps. Unreleased section always appears first.
any project using pm-changelog --group-by release or --group-by milestone
Create items with older release having more recent updated_at than newer release items, then generate changelog with --group-by release
Added compareVersionHeadings and compareVersionStrings sort functions. Sections now sorted descending by version with Unreleased first.
Sections appear newest-first ordered by release version string
Sections appeared in order of most-recently-updated item, causing older releases to appear before newer ones

value.author: steve
value.text: Root cause: Map insertion order in groupSectionsByMetadata depended on item updated_at,not release version. Fix sorts sections by version string descending.

value.author: steve
value.text: Fixed in same session it was discovered. Closes GitHub issue #6.

value.author: steve
value.text: Map insertion order in groupSectionsByMetadata is driven by item sort order,not by section key order. Always sort sections explicitly after grouping.

message: Create issue for changelog section ordering bug

author: steve
message: Add reviewer, risk, confidence, release fields

patch.value: Fixed: sections now sorted descending by version string with Unreleased always first. All 18 tests pass.
